ground_truth.description: Photo of Monolitten in Frogner Parken, Oslo. Monolitten is only recognizable part, should be obvious with and without metadata.
ground_truth.photographer_description: A photo of Monolitten (The Monolith) in Frognerparken, Oslo. This is part of Vigeland Sculpture Park. Monolitten is the only recognizable part of this photo, so if it doesn't get this, it would be hard to understand the rest. But it should be obvious both with and without metadata. Daytime, clear weather, summer/spring.

entities.name: Monolitten
entities.notes: The Monolith

details.description: A very tall granite column composed of intricately interwoven human figures rises above a canopy of trees under a bright, partly cloudy sky. The column stands on a circular base at the top of a stepped terrace. Around it are many pale granite figure groups and visitors on the platform, with a dark cast-iron lamp visible to the right. In the distance are low apartment blocks and hills. The scene is characteristic of the Monolith Plateau in the Vigeland Sculpture Park (Frogner Park) in Oslo.

details.description: A tall, cylindrical granite sculpture column carved with densely packed, intertwining human figures rises from a circular pedestal. The column, known as the Monolith, is surrounded by numerous additional stone sculptures of nude figures in various dynamic poses, forming a dense cluster around the base. The setting is a broad green park with trimmed shrubs and trees, under a bright blue sky with scattered clouds. The composition suggests a monumental sculpture installation set within Vigeland Park (Frognerparken) in Oslo, with clear views of the park’s sculptural ensemble extending into the distance.

details.description: A vertical outdoor photograph capturing The Monolith (Monolitten), a towering granite column composed of 121 intertwined human figures rising towards the sky. The sculpture stands prominently above a dense line of green trees and bushes. Through the foliage at the base, glimpses of other granite statue groups from the surrounding plateau are visible, depicting various human forms. To the left in the background, a multi-story reddish-brown residential building with white windows is visible. To the far right, a black lamp post stands among the crowd. The sky is a vibrant blue filled with dramatic, fluffy white and grey cumulus clouds.

details.description: A tall, vertical stone monolith intricately carved with 121 intertwined human figures climbing towards the sky. The column is made of a single piece of granite and stands as the centerpiece of a larger sculpture installation. It is situated on a circular plateau surrounded by several other granite sculpture groups depicting various stages of human life. The entire scene is set within a lush green park with dense foliage in the foreground and a modern residential building visible in the distance under a bright blue sky with scattered white and grey clouds.

Surrounding the monument are additional sculpted figures at ground level, with dense green shrubbery in the foreground. The scene is outdoors under a bright sky with scattered clouds, located in a large park known for its sculptural installations in Oslo, Norway.

details.description: A tall, single granite column composed of intertwined human figures rises above tree canopies under a partly cloudy sky. This is the prominent sculptural obelisk known as the Monolith, set on a plinth and encircled by terraces populated with additional white/grey granite figure groups visible at right. The scene is within a large urban park; a mid‑rise residential building is distant to the right. Lighting suggests daylight with crisp contrast, and the vantage appears slightly south of the monument, looking toward the Monolith Plateau within the Vigeland Sculpture Park in Oslo.

details.description: A tall vertical sculpture composed of numerous nude human figures wrapped around a central shaft, forming a continuous column. The figures ascend in a twisting, crowded procession from base to tip. The monument rests on a circular pedestal within a landscaped park, with dense greenery and several white-colored statues positioned around the base. In the background, a brick building peeks through the trees under a blue sky with scattered clouds, contributing to the park-like setting. The overall style suggests a monumental, commemorative sculpture piece by a prominent Nordic artist.

details.description: A photograph, oriented horizontally but depicting a vertical subject (rotated 90 degrees counter-clockwise), showing 'The Monolith' (Monolitten) by Gustav Vigeland. The subject is a towering granite column composed of 121 intertwined human figures carved in relief, rising towards the sky. The background features a vibrant blue sky filled with dramatic, textured white and grey clouds. On the right side of the frame (which corresponds to the ground level in the physical scene), lush green foliage from trees in Frogner Park is visible, along with glimpses of other granite sculptures from the surrounding installation and a distant brick building.

details.description: A tall, monolithic granite column known as 'The Monolith' (Monolitten) stands prominently against a dramatic, cloudy blue sky. The column is intricately carved with 121 human figures climbing and clinging to one another, spiraling upwards. It is situated on the highest point of a sculpture park, surrounded by other granite sculpture groups depicting various stages of human life. Lush green trees frame the scene on the right, and a modern building is partially visible in the background. The lighting is bright daylight, casting soft shadows on the textured stone surfaces.
